body:
# Bug report

## Current behavior

`Menu.Root` (default `modal=true`) does not reliably block outside interaction for a normal dropdown menu.

In my case (avatar menu in a fixed sidebar), opening the menu still allows clicks on outside sidebar controls.

## Expected behavior

When `Menu.Root` is modal (default behavior), outside interaction should be blocked while the menu is open.

## Reproducible example

https://gist.github.com/lyzno1/d36a9dbb904d33268f570f2e9a651854

## Base UI version

v1.1.0

## Which browser are you using?

Chrome (latest)

## Which OS are you using?

macOS

## Which assistive tech are you using (if applicable)?

N/A

## Additional context

I found an implementation mismatch that seems related:

- `MenuStore`/`MenuRoot` model `modal` as a real state (default `true`).
- `MenuPopup` passes `modal={isContextMenu}` to `FloatingFocusManager` instead of using the menu store's `modal` value.

Relevant files in this repo:

- `packages/react/src/menu/store/MenuStore.ts`
- `packages/react/src/menu/root/MenuRoot.tsx`
- `packages/react/src/menu/popup/MenuPopup.tsx`

Additionally, `InternalBackdrop` is fixed but has no explicit `z-index` (`packages/react/src/utils/InternalBackdrop.tsx`).
If the app has fixed chrome with a higher z-index (e.g. sidebar `z-index: 30`), outside controls can remain clickable above the backdrop.

So the observed behavior appears to come from a combination of:

1. `FloatingFocusManager` not getting menu `modal` state for non-context menus.
2. Backdrop stacking order not guaranteed against app chrome with higher z-index.

If you'd prefer, I can split this into two separate issues (modal propagation vs backdrop stacking) with separate minimal repros.

comments.body:
@lyzno1 I [pasted your gist in stackblitz](https://stackblitz.com/edit/fzhnypui?file=src%2FApp.tsx) and can't reproduce the issue:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/eb84aec5-7139-4ac0-962b-ac53bef7c852

@mj12albert I use it locally.
```
npm create vite@latest base-ui-menu-repro -- --template react-ts
cd base-ui-menu-repro
npm install && npm install @base-ui/react@1.1.0
npm run dev
```
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c80c4711-10ec-4ca9-96a9-72200cd292d8

> Backdrop stacking order not guaranteed against app chrome with higher z-index.

The `modal` prop creates a `position: fixed` backdrop that covers the entire page contents, but for this to work all the time, [`isolation: isolate` must be set on the app/root container](https://base-ui.com/react/overview/quick-start#portals), which avoids z-index issues/wars — then the `modal` backdrop always covers everything

> FloatingFocusManager not getting menu modal state for non-context menus.

This isn't related here, the `modal` prop intentionally doesn't trap focus for non-dialog elements

> > Backdrop stacking order not guaranteed against app chrome with higher z-index.
> 
> The `modal` prop creates a `position: fixed` backdrop that covers the entire page contents, but for this to work all the time, [`isolation: isolate` must be set on the app/root container](https://base-ui.com/react/overview/quick-start#portals), which avoids z-index issues/wars — then the `modal` backdrop always covers everything
> 
> > FloatingFocusManager not getting menu modal state for non-context menus.
> 
> This isn't related here, the `modal` prop intentionally doesn't trap focus for non-dialog elements

Thank you for your guidance, this issue has been resolved.

title: Menu.Root modal behavior does not fully block outside interaction for dropdowns
